title: Drug applications with unanswered queries to be rejected within 30 days
summary: The message was conveyed through a public notice issued by The Central Drugs Standard Control Organisation (CDSCO) on May 4. It said applications have been pending at the applicant's end since 2016.
thbAction: Position THB's pharma CRM and omnichannel HCP engagement workflows to support launch planning, KAM orchestration, and compliant follow-up across India and the Middle East.
contentHtml:
New Delhi: India's drug regulator has warned pharmaceutical companies that their drug approval applications will be rejected if they fail to answer queries within 30 days.

The message was conveyed through a public notice issued by The Central Drugs Standard Control Organisation (CDSCO) on May 4. It said applications have been pending at the applicant's end since 2016.

The notice covers divisions including gene therapy (GCT), bioavailability and bioequivalence (BABE), ethics committee, and veterinary segments, which have seen a significant pile-up of unanswered queries from pharmaceutical companies.

"A series of three reminders have been sent to the applicants, asking them to submit responses to the pending queries within the prescribed timelines," it said. "It has been observed that applications across various divisions are pending at the applicant's end due to non-submission of response to queries raised."

According to notice, in order to reduce the backlog of long-pending applications awaiting query responses across various divisions....the applicants who fail to respond to these queries through email and the SUGAM portal even after issuance of the three reminders are subsequently issued a fourth and final communication, informing them that their application stands rejected, with the fee amount forfeited, in accordance with the applicable provisions".

The drug regulator said the applications pending with applicants for more than two years have already been notified through the three-reminder process. They shall be rejected after a period of 30 days from the date of issue of this public notice. A final rejection communication will be issued accordingly to the applicants concerned.
